self_grade.calibration_note: Theory is B-grade (well-sourced), operational translations are C/D-grade (logical but unvalidated). Conservative -0.5 adjustment for speculation risk.

workshop_summary.purpose: Workshop deliverables, key findings, integration with IonWave, next steps

opkit_theoretical_operations.purpose: OK-M39-001: Generalizable framework for grounding Trade operations in theory (Alexander, Bohm, Tononi, Ashby)

what_worked: Theory-practice bridge: Translating deep theory into concrete protocols achieved goal
Confidence calibration: A/B/C/D grading + [VOID — requires X] maintained intellectual honesty
Pattern language approach: Alexander's methodology for extracting reusable patterns proved effective
Philosophical grounding: M39 provides 'why' layer that strengthens thesis and strategy
what_didnt_work: Speculation risk: Some concepts (love as MI, organizational consciousness) may appear too abstract/academic
Protocol overload: 6 protocols + 4 trust dimensions + 15 properties = high cognitive load
Reusability unknown: All protocols experimental, no cross-Trade validation yet
Deployment unclear: Roadmap exists, but prioritization weak (which protocols deploy first?)
key_insights: Theory IS valuable (provides pattern languages that compress complexity), but must translate to practice
Confidence grading is non-negotiable (D-grade claims marked as D, not inflated—intellectual honesty builds trust)
Pattern language methodology works (Alexander's structure for extracting reusable patterns is effective)
Risk of over-theorizing exists (M39 could become distraction—must balance theory with execution)
Validation is next frontier (all protocols need empirical testing, cross-Trade application, outcome measurement)

personas_planned: Philosophy Professor (challenges theoretical rigor, asks for citations, questions business applicability)
Pragmatic Operator (skeptical of theory, demands concrete value, wants ROI)
Systems Thinker (bridges theory and practice, identifies emergent properties, suggests connections)

hypothesis_cross_references.note: Will check if M39 theoretical foundations support or challenge existing hypotheses in registry
